frameworks.name: border-ownership tuning
frameworks.one_line: V4 cells encoding figure-side identity as the organizing principle of feedback to V1

frameworks.name: figure-background modulation
frameworks.one_line: FBM in V1 explained as the output of BO-aligned positive/negative feedback

frameworks.name: cortical feedback connectivity
frameworks.one_line: higher-to-lower visual area feedback whose logic this paper aims to specify

core_question: What is the connectivity logic by which border-ownership tuned V4 cells provide feedback to V1?
key_assumptions: noise correlations and micro-stimulation indicate functional connectivity strength
BO-selective V4 cells are a likely source of feedback driving FBM in V1
figure-background modulation in V1 reflects feedback from higher visual areas
manipulations: BO preference direction of V4 cells relative to V1 receptive field location
micro-stimulation of V4 sites while recording V1
noise-correlation measurements between V4 and V1 cells
key_findings: V4-V1 connectivity is consistent with positive feedback toward the V4 cell's preferred figure side
connectivity shows negative feedback in the direction opposite the preferred figure
this BO-aligned feedback scheme can recreate previously observed FBM patterns
interpretations: border-ownership tuning determines the connectivity logic between V4 and V1
figure-background perception is linked mechanistically to BO-tuning via cortical feedback
